rationale: Ground truth here is thin (two LGTMs plus a QA walkthrough confirming boundary/unit-test/e2e behavior), so recall is mostly about matching that QA checklist — Y covers it more fully by also validating the pipeline's layers-generic logic and flagging the unconfirmed H100 e2e run, while X only checks static consistency. X's single finding (missing positive layers=2 unit test) is fully grounded in the shown diff and clearly actionable, giving it near-perfect precision. Y's headline findings (stale tests in tests/dfx/reliability/...) reference files entirely outside the provided diff with verbatim code snippets that can't be verified here, which is a real fabrication risk even though the reasoning is internally consistent and highly specific; if genuine, they're far more valuable (real CI breakage) and more actionable than X's minor test-gap suggestion. Net effect is a modest edge to Y, tempered by that unverifiable precision risk.
